'''Maddie Macatten''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] who was considered to be old by [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Formerly a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Macatten was freed when her [[Maddie Macatten's master|master]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Aing-Tii/Legends|Aing-Tii]]—a mysterious [[Species/Legends|species]] that hated slavers—while on a trading mission. Taking one of her former master's [[Starship/Legends|ships]], the ''[[Scupper Bantoo]]'', Macatten headed into the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]]. Setting herself up as a [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]], she spent her [[Standard day/Legends|days]] searching for the [[Aing-Tii homeworld]] so that she could properly thank them for freeing her from slavery.

==Biography==
Maddie Macatten was an elderly [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. When her [[Maddie Macatten's master|master]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Aing-Tii/Legends|Aing-Tii]]—a [[Species/Legends|race]] noted for their hatred of slavers—Macatten found herself released from her servitude. Taking the ownership papers to one of her master's [[Starship/Legends|ships]], the [[Gymsnor-2 light freighter]] ''[[Scupper Bantoo]]'', she flew the vessel out of its [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]]. Macatten headed for the [[Kathol Outback/Legends|Kathol Outback]] and set herself up as a [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]], using the ''Scupper Bantoo'' to travel between the worlds of the Outback where she traded replacement machinery parts and luxury items to the various colonies she encountered. Macatten made several modifications to the ''Scupper Bantoo'', but it was an old vessel and could only take so much upgrading.<ref name="ko" />

While engaged in her trading endeavors, Macatten sought out any and all information on the Aing-Tii. She hoped to find their hidden [[Aing-Tii homeworld|homeworld]] so that she could personally thank them from freeing her from slavery. While a lot of the data she gained was rumor and speculation, Macatten was considered a good source of information on the Aing-Tii by the residents of the Outback. She also knew that the planet of [[Shatuun]] was home to a pair of mated [[Duinuogwuin/Legends|Star Dragons]], a fact that was known to only a few individuals. During her travels, Macatten encountered the crew of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'',<ref name="ko" /> which was on a mission to track down the rogue [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]], [[Kentor Sarne]].<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Maddie Macatten was a [[Color/Legends|black]]-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] Bith who possessed an impressive visual acuity when objects were within a thirty [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeter]] range, although her eyesight suffered when trying to focus on objects over twenty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] distant. She could not see anything beyond forty meters. Macatten could also smell the scent of individuals within a three-meter radius that helped her to determine their moods and state. She was also manually dexterous, and her fine motor skills assisted her while operating fine tools, or when pickpocketing.<ref name="ko" />

Macatten was slightly senile, constantly talking to herself, although she rarely finished a sentence. She had a tendency to ramble on about anything and everything when in the presence of others.<ref name="ko" /> Some even considered her crazy.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|272|Macatten, Maddie}}</ref> Although she had a lot of information to share, most of it was wrong. Despite this, elements of the truth were often found in her words. The streams of babble usually bored those present, causing them to stop listening to her before any useful information was revealed. Macatten would often [[Music/Legends|hum]] Bith folk tunes to herself while working, and her priority in life was the discovery of the Aing-Tii homeworld so that she could thank them for indirectly freeing her from slavery.<ref name="ko" />

Macatten had the ability to [[Pilot/Legends|fly]], repair, and modify [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]], as well as [[Astrogation/Legends|navigate]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. An experienced trader and businessbeing, she was a shrewd bargainer and could ascertain the value of items. Macatten knew a lot of information regarding the Kathol Outback, although not all of it was accurate, and she knew about [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species, cultures and languages, and was quite streetwise despite her burgeoning senility. She was also a deft pickpocket and con artist when the need arose, and was know to [[Gambling/Legends|gamble]]. Despite her advanced years, Macatten was still relatively fit and could climb and jump, and was agile enough to avoid danger when necessary. Technically adept, she could also drive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles, program and repair [[Computer/Legends|computers]] and [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and apply [[Medic/Legends|first aid]]. She was skilled with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], and armed herself with a [[Sporting blaster/Legends|sporting model]]. She also carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], various tools, and 250 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="ko" />

==Behind the scenes==
Maddie Macatten was created for ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'', a supplement to [[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|''The DarkStryder Campaign'']] published by [[West End Games]]. She was intended to be used by the Gamemaster to introduce information—or even ''mis''information—to the players regarding the Aing-Tii species, as well as to serve as a source of potential replacement parts. It is also suggested that Macatten could join the crew of the ''FarStar'', the player character's principal ship, should the Gamemaster so wish.
